So what is debt? If you talk to most people they're terrified of debt. The people that you're talking to busted out. They took on debt without the income to support it. When we think about debt in our society we often think about the mounting credit card bills, out of control mortgages and upside down deals that bankrupt good people. We think about college debt at $1.3 trillion. Credit card debt is just a little below that. What was that far? You borrowed money for an education, you borrowed money for consumable items, you borrowed money for a house to live in, you borrowed money for a car to drive around and none of it produced income. Look debt is an enormous responsibility. It can get out of hand. It can derail a great deal if you take on too much leverage. It can blow your budget if you're not careful. At the same time, debt is a tremendous leverage tool that enables the smart, savvy real estate investor to realize massive opportunities today instead of years or even decades down the road. By utilizing debt correctly you can build your business. When I say your business I'm talking about a collection of properties not one property and you can build long-term wealth in a fraction of the time. Look if you're going to get involved in investing in apartments you're taking on debt. If you can't embrace that concept do not play this game because you're not going to last. You need debt. Debt is good. Let me give you an example. Let's say that you go out and grab a deal that requires $12 million worth of debt. Now the person that helped me put this program together suggested that you were going to get a loan for $1.2 million. There's no deals that will work for you that require $1.2 million of debt. You have to look at deals bigger than that. So let's just use an example. You're going to take on $12 million worth of debt and that debt is going to cost you four and a half percent. Even if you're only paying interest for the first five years, interest only loan, you're still carrying an annual debt load in this case of about $540,000 per year or $45,000 per month. Remember always figure out your numbers annually not just monthly. Have both. When you layer in all the other debts tied to your property, what do you mean other debts? See I hear everybody say I don't want to do debt. I don't want any financing. I don't want to use mortgages. I don't want to use the banks. How many different debts do you think you have right now? Financing is only one debt. How about property taxes? Debt. How about vendors that you owe to fix the floors? Are you going to do everything? That's debt. How about you got to go get some money, borrow money to do a rehab on the property in five years because you need to improve the property to bring it to market? That's going to be debt. Those are obligations. How about all the employees that you have to pay every month? That is an obligation and a debt. How about those two kids, those freeloaders at your house that you're going to send to schools or colleges? It's going to cost a million and a half dollars to send both the Rugrats to schools where they will learn nothing and probably have to move back into your house. Look there's a lot of debt out there. Don't be scared of financing. You will need it to fund your deals. You will need it to get the big deals. You will need it to put you and your investors in a situation where you can actually have cash flow. Write down all the interest on your loans by the way. This is the best debt in the world because if I borrow 12 million dollars and pay 540,000 dollars a year in interest, all 540,000 dollars is deductible. For some investors, debt is scary. Get over it. Grow up. This is not your mommy and daddy's economy nor is it your mom, your dad, your uncle or some other little flipper, wholesaler guy giving you advice how to play the real real estate game. What if something goes wrong you think? Dude, what if something goes wrong? What if something goes wrong? You lost your down payment. That's what you should be worried about. Not I lost the loan. What if the market tanks? Yeah, what if? If the queen had wings, she'd be a bumblebee or something like that. What if the market tanks? If the market tanks, everything tanks and all the big players are going to the bank saying, hey, let's renegotiate the debt. What if there's some massive expense on your property and you can't cover your debt? Well, then your underwriting was wrong and you picked the wrong property. Look, debt in the case of multifamily real estate is awesome. It's wonderful. It's good. As we discussed earlier, your DCR, remember that? Debt coverage ratio is a critical calculation that will help you handle your fears surrounding this debt. But I wouldn't go to sleep at night worried about my debt if I know I have a great deal. I go to sleep at night and say, man, I have a great deal in Orlando. I just closed it. I got a great deal in Miami. We're closing on. I got this great deal I'm working on in Houston, Texas. I got these three beautiful deals in Phoenix that I love. Four more deals in Miami. I'm getting ready to put down. I don't think about the debt. I never think about the debt because I got good debt. I got debt that is being paid down every month. Either the interest is being paid in full or the interest and the principle of being paid down and my debt will be paid down to zero until I refinance the property again and take on more debt. You need to make sure in your due diligence and your analysis and your underwriting, in the worst case scenario, do you cover your debt coverage? Does your debt coverage ratio your DCR work out? In other words, make sure if everything bust and again, this is why I said use worst case scenarios. The only people that are scared of debt or people that are careless, can you cover your debt in a worst case scenario is so pull the trigger. Do you love the deal? Now we're back to the deal. Do you love the deal? Do you want to go to sleep at night saying, I own that address? Do you want to drive there next week, next month, next year? Your debt's not the problem. Your debt is your friend. You're probably, if you can answer those questions, I love the deal. I love it next week. I love it next month. I love it next year. I love it more three years from now. I love it 10 years from now. Even more, you've got a solid deal, borrow money against it. Because you've taken on the debt, you can do incredible things. Consider this, a 5% cap rate on a $5 million building is $250,000. That means $5 million. If I paid cash for the building, my cap rate, okay, my capital rate of return, a 5 cap on a $150,000 building is $7,500. Think about the quarter of a million. Don't think about 7,500. It's pretty clear which deal you want to do to build wealth, the bigger deals. Also, if I put debt on that 5% deal, that 5% cap rate, and I can borrow money for 4, and I'm buying a $5 million deal, and let's say I borrow 4 million of the 5 million. $4 million, the difference between the interest rate on the 4 million and the cap rate on the 4 million is one point. I borrowed money at 4%, I bought it at 5%. I bought the deal at a 5 cap rate. I'm going to make one percentage point on $4 million. That's not even my money. That is genius. And the difference between the interest rate and the cap rate means a bigger cash flow for you and your investors. And hopefully, if you pick the right deal, more appreciation in the future.
